primary_concern: Sepsis risk with concurrent acute kidney injury and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) combined with leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) and thrombocytopenia (88-95 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for sepsis risk. Concurrent AKI Stage 3 (excluded outlier creatinine 8.9 mg/dL) and persistent platelets <100 K/uL indicate multi-organ dysfunction. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (9.8 g/dL) suggest possible hepatic involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
Creatinine elevation meets KDIGO Stage 1 (excluded outlier indicates progression to Stage 3)
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or ≥1.5x baseline

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulation scoring threshold
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate broad-spectrum antibiotics per local sepsis protocol
Obtain blood cultures and infection source investigation
Start fluid resuscitation and reassess perfusion status
Monitor renal function with hourly urine output
Order coagulation panel (INR, PT/TT) given thrombocytopenia
Consider vasopressor support if hypotension develops
doctor_handoff: Review infection source (imaging/cultures), assess response to initial resuscitation, and evaluate for ICU admission if not already present.

disease_progression.observation: Initial sepsis markers: WBC 14.2, Hgb 9.8, Bilirubin 1.4
Lactate rises to 4.2 mmol/L with platelets 88 K/uL

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.

family_communication.english_summary: In the last 12 hours, we've seen signs that your loved one is fighting a serious infection (sepsis) affecting multiple organs. Their blood tests show elevated lactate (a sign of poor tissue perfusion), high white blood cells, and low platelets. Kidney function is severely impacted (AKI Stage 3), and there are early signs their liver might also be involved. The care team has started broad-spectrum antibiotics, fluids, and is closely monitoring kidney function. We're checking for the infection's source and will continue to watch for any drop in blood pressure that might require additional support.
family_communication.regional_summary: पिछले 12 घंटों में हमें लगा है कि आपके प्रिय के शरीर में गंभीर संक्रमण (सिप्सिस) है जो कई अंगों को प्रभावित कर रहा है। उनके ब्लड टेस्ट में लैक्टेट बढ़ा हुआ है (जो ऊतकों को खराब रक्त प्रवाह का संकेत देता है), सफेद रक्त कोशिकाएं अधिक हैं, और प्लेटलेट्स कम हैं। गुर्दे का काम बुरी तरह से प्रभावित हुआ है (एकी स्टेज 3), और यह भी लगता है कि यकृत प्रभावित हो सकता है। चिकित्सा टीम व्यापक एंटीबायोटिक्स, तरल पदार्थ, और गुर्दे के काम की निगरानी शुरू कर चुकी है। हम संक्रमण के स्रोत की जांच कर रहे हैं और रक्तचाप में कमी के लिए निरंतर निगरानी करेंगे।
